channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Shortage of specialised teachers leaves Grade 10 STEM learners untaught
item.description: Schools across Kenya are struggling with a serious shortage of specialised teachers, particularly in technical subjects under the STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Mathematics) pathway. Grade 10...

item.title: MILF urges prompt appointment of new government peace panel chairman
item.description: COTABATO CITY — The Moro Islamic Liberation Front (MILF) has temporarily paused interactions with its government counterpart in the peace implementing panel following the resignation of retired Army G...

item.title: Boko Haram Demands N3.52 Billion Ransom for 176 Woro Residents as Government Talks Stall
item.description: Dem 176 people from Woro community, Kaiama LGA, Kwara State, don dey held by Boko Haram, and the terrorists don ask N20 million per person, totalling N3.52 billion. The state government dey waka for n...

item.title: SAPS divided over Mchunu's directive to disband political killings task team
item.description: Suspended police minister Senzo Mchunu’s directive to immediately disband the Political Killings Task Team (PKTT) has caused significant divisions within the South African Police Service (SAPS). Natio...

item.title: Two 23-year-olds to face murder charges after Eldorado Park shooting leaves two dead
item.description: Police in Gauteng have arrested two 23-year-old men in connection with a shooting in Eldorado Park, Johannesburg, that claimed the lives of two people and injured six others over the weekend. The susp...

item.title: Impact of Invasive Species on Carnivores in the Thar Desert
item.description: The Thar Desert, spanning western India and parts of Pakistan, is undergoing significant ecological changes due to invasive species and human influence. A recent ecological study highlights the impact...

item.title: Matiang’i Counters Ruto’s Comments on Diet, Urges Focus on National Issues
item.description: Former Interior Cabinet Secretary Fred Matiang’i has hit back at President William Ruto following remarks about opposition leaders' eating habits. Ruto, speaking in Chwele, Bungoma county, said he had...

item.title: Prince William joins radio host on tandem bike to support charity
item.description: Prince William recently surprised a British radio host by joining him on a tandem bicycle ride to raise funds for charity. The event took place in a light-hearted and spontaneous atmosphere, highlight...

item.title: Researchers Identify How Lecanemab Activates Microglia to Clear Alzheimer’s Plaques
item.description: Researchers at the Vlaams Instituut voor Biotechnologie (VIB) and KU Leuven have uncovered the mechanism by which the Alzheimer’s drug lecanemab, marketed as Leqembi, works in the brain. The study rev...

item.title: Met Office forecasts up to 20C in parts of UK on Wednesday, warmest day of 2026 so far
item.description: Forecasters at the Met Office have indicated that temperatures in the United Kingdom could reach as high as 20°C on Wednesday, marking what is likely to be the warmest day of the year thus far. This s...

item.title: PM Modi and UAE President Discuss West Asia Situation, Condemn Attacks and Commit to Regional Peace and Strait of Hormuz Safety
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi held a telephonic conversation with UAE President Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan on Tuesday amid escalating tensions in West Asia. PM Modi conveyed advance Eid greetin...

item.title: Fredericton police arrest two individuals following weapons-related call
item.description: Fredericton police responded to a call regarding weapons on March 17, 2026, resulting in the arrest of two individuals. The details surrounding the incident have not been fully disclosed, but authorit...

item.title: Essendon defender refutes claims of laziness amid losing streak
item.description: Essendon defender Ben McKay has pushed back against criticism from former player Matthew Lloyd, insisting the Bombers are far from lazy despite their current on-field struggles. The team recently suff...

item.title: Taliban report heavy civilian casualties after airstrike on Kabul drug rehabilitation hospital
item.description: At least 400 people were reported killed and 250 injured in an airstrike on the Omid drug rehabilitation hospital in Kabul, Afghanistan, according to a spokesman for the Afghan Taliban government. The...

item.title: Torcross villagers express disappointment after exclusion from £1.4bn government coastal and flood defence funding
item.description: Residents of Torcross and nearby Beesands in South Devon are facing significant challenges following severe damage caused by Storm Imogen in February 2026. The storm washed away a 200-metre section of...

item.title: 14 detained in Varanasi over Iftar on Ganga after complaint regarding religious sentiments
item.description: In Varanasi, Uttar Pradesh, fourteen individuals were taken into custody following allegations of defiling a place of worship and hurting religious feelings. The arrests followed the viral circulation...

item.title: Magufuli’s Legacy Reflected in Tanzanians’ Work Ethic and Community Spirit

item.title: Free Health Screening for Aviation Staff to Boost Safety Standards
item.description: Aviation staff in Tanzania soon go get free health check-ups under new programme designed to improve safety by spotting medical problems early that fit affect their work performance. The screenings, a...
